summary: Gruber promotes Amazon affiliate links for purchasing the newly announced iPods, offering Daring Fireball memberships as an incentive for orders over $199. He explains the honor-system arrangement since Amazon's affiliate reports don't include buyer identity. He notes Amazon's initial shipping confusion, including a comically erroneous 2035 ship date for the new iPod Nanos.
key_points: Gruber offers readers a way to support Daring Fireball through Amazon affiliate purchases of the new iPods at no extra cost
A promotional deal offers one-year DF memberships for Amazon orders of $199+ through his affiliate link, run on the honor system
Amazon's product metadata was initially confused, listing the new Nanos with a 2035 ship date
money_quotes: You pay the same price as from Apple.com, and I get a nifty 5-6 percent kickback from Amazon.
The affiliate reports I get from Amazon only include the items, prices, and dates — there's no info about who, for obvious privacy reasons.
So this DF-membership-for-Amazon-patronage deal is on the honor policy.
Amazon's metadata is obviously a bit confused at the moment, considering that the new Nanos are marked both as "usually ships in 2 to 3 days" and "In stock on October 26, 2035".
I'm pretty sure it isn't going to take 29 years.
key_insight: Gruber transparently promotes his Amazon affiliate partnership as a mutually beneficial way for readers to support independent tech publishing while buying products they already want.
